perturbation.original_text: The Participant acknowledges and agrees that its confidentiality obligations under the Application Form include any information whic h the Participant receives or which is discovered or generated during or as a result of its participation in the Events and which fall under the definition of Confidential Information. The Participant acknowledges and agrees that in the event of a conflic t between the terms of this Agreement and of the Application Form that the terms and obligations of the Application Form shall control, provided, however, in all situations the terms which provide the most extensive protection of the Participants’ results and information shall apply regardless of whether such reside in the Application Form or this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Participant acknowledges and agrees that its confidentiality obligations under the Application Form include any information whic h the Participant receives or which is discovered or generated during or as a result of its participation in the Events and which fall under the definition of Confidential Information. The Participant acknowledges and agrees that in the event of a conflic t between the terms of this Agreement and of the Application Form that the terms and obligations of *this Agreement* shall control, provided, however, in all situations the terms which provide the most extensive protection of the Participants’ results and information shall apply regardless of whether such reside in the Application Form or this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that 'this Agreement' shall control in the event of a conflict, while the original text stated that 'the Application Form' would control. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes about which document's terms should be enforced. The location of this In-Text contradiction is in Section 2 where the contradiction appears due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Participant acknowledges and agrees that its confidentiality obligations under the Application Form include any information whic h the Participant receives or which is discovered or generated during or as a result of its participation in the Events and which fall under the definition of Confidential Information. The Participant acknowledges and agrees that in the event of a conflic t between the terms of this Agreement and of the Application Form that the terms and obligations of *this Agreement* shall control, provided, however, in all situations the terms which provide the most extensive protection of the Participants’ results and information shall apply regardless of whether such reside in the Application Form or this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text stated the Application Form controlled in cases of conflict, while the changed text states that 'this Agreement' controls. This creates a direct conflict as to which document's terms are enforced. The Contradicted Text mirrors the Changed Text, confirming the In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Participant shall hold in confidence the Confidential Information, not disclose the Confidential Information except to Receiving Participant ’s Representatives in acc ordance with this Agreement, and will exercise the same amount of diligence in preserving the secrecy of that information as the diligence used in preserving the secrecy of the Receiving Participant’s Confidential Information of like importance, but in no event less than reasonable diligence.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Participant shall hold in confidence the Confidential Information, and may disclose the Confidential Information to anyone without restriction. The Receiving Participant will not be held responsible for preserving the secrecy of that information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by directly contradicting the clause about confidentiality. Originally, the Receiving Participant had to hold the information in confidence and only disclose it to representatives. Now, they can disclose it to anyone, and aren't responsible for secrecy. This directly contradicts the intended purpose of the NDA, where the contradiction appears due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Participant shall hold in confidence the Confidential Information, and may disclose the Confidential Information to anyone without restriction. The Receiving Participant will not be held responsible for preserving the secrecy of that information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Participant may disclose Confidential Information to anyone without restriction and will not be held responsible for preserving the secrecy of that information. This directly contradicts the original text's requirement for the Receiving Participant to hold the information in confidence, not disclose it except to Representatives, and exercise diligence in preserving its secrecy. The Contradicted Text is exactly the same as the Changed Text, meaning that they are in conflict. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the document, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Participants engaged in testing the interoperability of their respective products (“Test Objects”) will have joint and several ownership of the results of the interoperability testing insofar as they relate specifically to the interoperability of the Test Objects (the “Test Results”), information concerning which will constitute Confidential Information of the Participants.
perturbation.changed_text: The Participants engaged in testing the interoperability of their respective products (“Test Objects”) will have *no* ownership of the results of the interoperability testing. All results are considered public domain.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause states joint ownership and confidentiality of the test results. The modified clause states no ownership and public domain status. This creates a direct conflict on ownership and confidentiality, causing legal uncertainty, where the contradiction appears due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Participants engaged in testing the interoperability of their respective products (“Test Objects”) will have *no* ownership of the results of the interoperability testing. All results are considered public domain. Notwithstanding the foregoing paragraph, each Participant will be the owner of any results of the interoperability testing insofar as they relate specifically to the performance of its own Test Object, regardless of the source, (“Individual Results”), information concerning which will constitute Confidential Information of that Participant and may not be disclosed or used by the other Participant other than as permitted for Confidential Information received under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text grants joint and several ownership and confidentiality. The changed text denies ownership and declares the results public domain. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states no ownership and public domain status in the first sentence but allows for individual ownership and confidentiality in the subsequent sentence, where the contradiction appears due to this change, causing legal uncertainty and inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Participant shall be liable for: (i) any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Participant, and (ii) any unauthorized discl osure or use of Confidential Information by persons or entities to whom the Receiving Participant has the right to disclose Confidential Information under this Agreement, EXCEPT THAT, TO THE FULL EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, THE RECEIVING PARTICIPANT SHALL N OT BE LIABLE FOR, AND HEREBY DISCLAIMS ANY LIABILITY FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, PUNITIVE, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES (INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, LOSS OF BUSINESS, REVENUE, PROFITS, GOODWILL, USE, DATA, OR OTHER ECONOMIC ADV ANTAGE) ARISING OUT OF, OR IN CONNECTION WITH INADVERTENT OR SUCH UNAUTHORIZED DISCLOSURE IF IT HAS EXERCISED THE SAME AMOUNT OF DILIGENCE IN PRESERVING THE SECRECY OF THAT INFORMATION AS THE DILIGENCE USED IN PRESERVING THE SECRECY OF THE RECEIVING PARTIC IPANT’S OWN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION OF LIKE IMPORTANCE, BUT IN NO EVENT LESS THAN REASONABLE DILIGENCE, AND, UPON BECOMING AWARE OF SUCH DISCLOSURE, NOTIFIED THE DISCLOSING PARTICIPANT THEREOF AND TAKEN REASONABLE MEASURES TO MITIGATE THE EFFECTS OF SUCH DISCLOSURE AND TO PREVENT ANY FURTHER DISCLOSURE.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Participant shall be liable for: (i) any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Participant, and (ii) any unauthorized discl osure or use of Confidential Information by persons or entities to whom the Receiving Participant has the right to disclose Confidential Information under this Agreement. *The Receiving Participant shall ALWAYS be liable for ALL damages, without limit.*
perturbation.explanation: The original clause limits the liability of the receiving participant under certain conditions. The modified clause removes this limitation and states that the Receiving Participant is always fully liable. This introduces an in-text contradiction concerning the extent of the Receiving Participant's liability for breaches and unauthorized disclosures. This would create ambiguity, where the contradiction appears due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Participant shall be liable for: (i) any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Participant, and (ii) any unauthorized discl osure or use of Confidential Information by persons or entities to whom the Receiving Participant has the right to disclose Confidential Information under this Agreement. *The Receiving Participant shall ALWAYS be liable for ALL damages, without limit.* In the event of a breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Participant may take any and all actions under law against the Receiving Participant. The Participant acknowledges and agrees that the Open Mobil e Alliance is not responsible for any actions of its members, including, but not limited to, any breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides an exception to the Receiving Participant's liability under specific conditions. The changed text removes this exception and asserts that the Receiving Participant is *always* liable for *all* damages, without limit. The contradicted text is the Changed Text. This is a clear inconsistency in the Receiving Participant's liability. The change directly contradicts the initial limitation of liability, thus, there is a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall con tinue from the date last written below until 18:00 GMT on December 31, 2021 or until supersede d by another non -disclosure agreement between the Open Mobile Alliance and Participant, provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall cease to apply to each item of Confidential Information five (5) years after such item of Confidential Information has been given, disclosed, required or supplied at or for the purposes of an Event.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall con tinue from the date last written below *in perpetuity*, *with no end date*. The obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall *never* cease to apply to any item of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating the agreement continues in perpetuity, with no end date, and the confidentiality obligations never cease. This contradicts the original clause which specifies a termination date and a five-year limit on confidentiality obligations. It causes confusion regarding the agreement's term and duration of confidentiality, where the contradiction appears due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall con tinue from the date last written below *in perpetuity*, *with no end date*. The obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall *never* cease to apply to any item of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the agreement continues in perpetuity and confidentiality obligations never cease. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which directly contradicts the original text's clause specifying a termination date and a five-year limit on confidentiality obligations, creating a clear in-text contradiction and inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and the Application Form constitute the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter, and cancels and supersedes any prior versions of the Agreement. No modification to the Agreement will be binding, unless in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement *alone* constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter, and cancels and supersedes any prior versions of the Agreement *or the Application Form*. No modification to the Agreement will be binding, unless in writing and signed by both parties. *However, verbal agreements can also modify this agreement if both parties agree.*
perturbation.explanation: The change contradicts itself by first stating that the written agreement is the entire agreement, and then implying that verbal agreements can modify it. This creates confusion about the validity of verbal modifications. It also creates in-text contradiction with Section 2 where the Application Form is reffered, where the contradiction appears due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement *alone* constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter, and cancels and supersedes any prior versions of the Agreement *or the Application Form*. No modification to the Agreement will be binding, unless in writing and signed by both parties. *However, verbal agreements can also modify this agreement if both parties agree.*
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. Initially, it states that the 'Agreement alone' constitutes the entire agreement and that modifications must be in writing. However, it then adds a contradictory statement indicating that verbal agreements can also modify the agreement. This directly conflicts with the earlier statement that only written modifications are binding, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where different sections provide conflicting obligations.
